metadata.story_title: Slim Ideation SKILL.md — Remove Architect Phases + Adopt Structured Output

observations.note: All 4 modified files form a cohesive documentation set with valid cross-references. SKILL.md → Phase 3 references artifact-generation.md and completion-handoff.md correctly. Self-validation workflow is properly integrated as Phase 3.3 checkpoint.
evidence: 52/52 tests passed validating cross-references
No broken reference links detected
Phase flow coherent: Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3

observations.note: Skill output contract properly updated: ideation produces requirements.md (YAML per F4 schema) instead of epic.md. F4 schema structure consistently referenced across artifact-generation.md, self-validation-workflow.md, and completion-handoff.md. Next action recommendation properly delegates to /create-epic (architecture skill).
evidence: completion-handoff.md lists requirements.md as primary artifact
artifact-generation.md specifies YAML requirements.md output format
F4 schema fields (functional_requirements, non_functional_requirements, constraints, dependencies) referenced in 3+ files
/create-epic mentioned as next action in completion handoff

observations.note: Architect phases cleanly removed without affecting retained phases. Old Phase 3 (Complexity Assessment), Phase 4 (Epic Decomposition), and Phase 5 (Feasibility Analysis) completely absent from SKILL.md and all phase definitions. Phase renumbering successful: former Phase 6 now Phase 3 (Requirements Output).
evidence: No 'Phase 3: Complexity Assessment' header in SKILL.md
No 'Phase 4' or 'Phase 5' headers in SKILL.md
Exactly 3 phases found in SKILL.md (Phase 1, 2, 3)
No references to complexity-assessment-workflow.md, epic-decomposition-workflow.md, or feasibility-analysis-workflow.md in phase definitions

observations.note: Self-validation workflow properly updated for PM-only (ideation) scope. Epic validation checks removed. Complexity score validation (0-60 range, tier 1-4) removed. Feasibility assessment validation removed. F4 schema validation added. Requirements.md schema validation retained and enhanced.
evidence: self-validation-workflow.md contains no references to epic validation
No 'complexity score' or '0-60' patterns in validation workflow
No 'feasibility assessment' validation documented
F4 schema validation and YAML structure checks present

observations.note: Reference files properly managed per BR-003: architect phase reference files retained for future migration (complexity-assessment-*.md, epic-decomposition-workflow.md, feasibility-*.md not deleted). Discovery and requirements-elicitation reference files remain unchanged and properly referenced.
evidence: discovery-workflow.md exists and referenced by Phase 1
requirements-elicitation-workflow.md exists and referenced by Phase 2
error-type-1, error-type-2, error-type-4 remain in error handling
Architect phase reference files retained (not deleted) for architecture skill migration

observations.note: 4 modified files maintain consistent documentation of skill scope and output. No conflicting statements about primary output (all reference requirements.md). F4 schema structure consistently defined. No legacy epic/complexity/feasibility responsibilities documented.
evidence: All 4 files mention requirements.md or F4 schema
Consistent terminology: 'PM role' (ideation), 'architect role' (architecture)
Next action consistently recommends /create-epic
F4 schema fields consistently referenced

observations.note: Integration test suite is comprehensive and validates actual component interactions rather than mocking. Tests use real file reads, regex pattern matching on actual content, and cross-file reference validation. No excessive mocking (mock ratio = 0). All assertions are concrete and testable.
evidence: 52 tests executed against real SKILL.md and reference files
Tests verify: phase headers, references, cross-links, schema compliance
No skip decorators or TODO placeholders in test code
Anti-gaming validation score: 100/100

observations.note: Clear handoff boundary established between ideation (PM role: discovery, elicitation, requirements) and architecture (Architect role: epic creation, complexity assessment, feasibility analysis). Ideation no longer produces epics; users invoke /create-epic after ideation. This separation of concerns aligns with Single Responsibility Principle.
evidence: completion-handoff.md explicitly recommends /create-epic
No epic generation code in artifact-generation.md
Complexity assessment explicitly delegated to architecture
Feasibility analysis explicitly delegated to architecture

test_classes.name: TestStory438SkillStructure
test_classes.purpose: Validate SKILL.md structural integrity after phase removal and file modifications

test_classes.name: TestCrossFileReferences
test_classes.purpose: Verify cross-references between SKILL.md and reference files are valid

test_classes.name: TestDocumentationConsistency
test_classes.purpose: Validate all 4 modified files form a consistent documentation set

test_classes.name: TestOperationalTreeConsistency
test_classes.purpose: Verify src/ and .claude/ trees are consistent

test_classes.name: TestPhaseRenumbering
test_classes.purpose: Validate Phase 6 was properly renumbered to Phase 3

critical_validations: All 7 ACs validated with 100% test passage
All cross-references between SKILL.md and reference files verified valid
Output contract updated (requirements.md per F4 schema)
